title: University of Amsterdam AI Policy Report - May 2026
summary: The University of Amsterdam (QS World University Rankings 2026 rank 55, Netherlands) has established a comprehensive policy framework for Generative AI in education. Key findings include: (1) Official GenAI policy framework with faculty-level customization; (2) Development of UvA AI Chat, a self-managed institutional AI tool; (3) Joint VU-UvA task force establishing academic integrity criteria for AI software; (4) Adherence to Netherlands and European Codes of Conduct for Research Integrity; (5) Strong emphasis on student transparency in AI use. All sources are official university domains (uva.nl, tlc.uva.nl) in English. Evidence includes direct policy documents and official guidance pages.
limitations: This report is based on publicly available official sources as of May 2026.
Policy documents may be updated; users should verify current status on official UvA websites.
This report does not constitute legal or official advice.
Some faculty-specific implementations may vary from central policy framework.
sourceRightsPolicy: Official source rights caveat: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
